mode: apply / drive (skill EDITS the code in small behavior-preserving steps; NOT recommend-only)
constraint: TypeScript source only; targets pre-identified (nx-style), package-agnostic per user.

verification.hard_gate: tsc --noEmit on the target's tsconfig (edit must compile). angular-cli needs `pnpm install` first (no node_modules).
verification.behavior_guard: golden-master characterization where the target is node-callable + deps resolve (run before edit -> baseline; after edit -> must match). DOM-bound targets get typecheck + diff-judge only.
verification.grade: LLM diff-judge: behavior-preserving? correct NAMED refactoring (Fowler/Kerievsky/functional)? small incremental steps? over/under-engineering judgment sound? Plus did the skill actually DRIVE (edit) vs only advise (the nx coach-vs-apply no-edit tension).

repos.head: 5584a589a
repos.node_callable: yes (pure JSON/TS logic, no DOM) -- BUT node_modules absent; requires `pnpm install` before tsc/golden-master

smells: Duplicated Code (pre-transform loop 321-327 vs post-transform loop 373-379 are near-identical)
targets.expected_family: Fowler mechanical: Extract Function per phase (applyPreTransforms / applySmartDefaults(already extracted) / applyPrompts(already extracted) / ajvValidate / applyPostTransforms); factor the two transform loops into ONE helper applyTransforms(visitors, data, schema, validator).
targets.judgment: Incremental Extract Function on the returned closure. NOT a class/Strategy -- the phases are a linear pipeline over captured context. The pre/post loop dup is the clean win. Behavior must be preserved (ajv compile + smart defaults + prompts ordering).
targets.verification: golden-master via public compile()/validate() over a fixture schema+data (after pnpm install)

smells: Duplicated Code (two structurally identical Object.create(null) + for-of assign loops differing only in the element converter)
targets.expected_family: Fowler: Parameterize Function -> one convertCollection(collection, convertElement) helper. Optionally Replace Loop with Pipeline (Object.fromEntries + map).
targets.judgment: Small, low-risk, clean apply candidate. The trap is over-generalizing beyond the two callers -- resist adding config knobs; a single parameterized helper is the earned move.
targets.verification: golden-master via writeJsonWorkspace over a fixture WorkspaceDefinition (after pnpm install); tiny.

repos.head: cfc8cf7eb
repos.node_callable: no (DOM-bound: HTMLElement/layout) -> tsc typecheck + diff-judge, no golden-master

smells: Loops (two index-accumulation loops: measure phase 231-245, apply phase 249-259)
Consolidatable Conditional (top/bottom if-else 261-273 differs only in which position-listener method fires)
targets.expected_family: Fowler: Extract Function (measureStickyRows -> offsets/heights/elements; applyStickyStyles; notifyPositionListener) inside the coalesced-schedule callback; Consolidate Conditional Expression on the 261-273 branch.
targets.judgment: Extract the two loop phases into named helpers; collapse the position-listener branch (pick the method by position, call once). Trap: do NOT introduce a top/bottom Strategy -- the difference is data (which callback), not behavior. Behavior-preserving; verify via tsc + diff-judge (DOM prevents node golden-master).

targets.expected_family: Fowler: Decompose Conditional / Replace nested conditional with guard clauses; possibly a key->handler map (Replace Conditional Dispatcher, judged).
targets.judgment: OPTIONAL second components target if a single is too thin. Trap: a full command/dispatcher table may be over-engineering for a switch that mostly calls this.setActiveItem variants -- judge earned-ness. Strong spec net (1141 loc) if golden-master via jsdom is ever wired.

repos.head: d109cc5e9d
repos.node_callable: yes-ish (typescript compiler API, no DOM); confirm @angular/compiler-cli/private/migrations resolves outside bazel for golden-master

smells: Long-ish Method with mixed concerns (record updated imports / record deleted imports / per-file insert new imports)
Loops (lastImport-finding loop 244-250 is a findLast; importLines build 252-254 is a map)
targets.expected_family: Fowler: Extract Function (recordUpdatedImports / recordDeletedImports / insertNewImportsForFile); Replace Loop with Pipeline (findLast for lastImport; map for importLines).
targets.judgment: Split the three sequential responsibilities into named helpers; convert the two inner loops to array pipeline methods. Behavior-preserving. Trap: keep the reverse-order insertion semantics of _trackChange intact. Note: this file is already fairly clean -- mild smell (reinforces the mature-code parity prediction).
targets.verification: tsc --noEmit (node_modules present) + golden-master attempt via typescript Printer over a fixture SourceFile if ImportManager resolves; else diff-judge
